items.title: Source Health Check with Status Indicators
items.description: Extend the InteractiveSourcesWidget with a per-source health check protocol. Each ingester should expose a lightweight probe (not a full ingest) that checks connectivity/path availability and returns a HealthStatus (ok, degraded, unavailable) plus a latency or last-seen timestamp. The TUI renders color-coded status dots next to each source and a Ctrl+R shortcut to re-probe all sources.
items.user_story: As a developer, I want to see at a glance which of my configured ingest sources are healthy, degraded, or broken in the TUI sources view, so I can diagnose issues without running a full ingest cycle.
steps: [DONE] Define a HealthStatus enum (OK, DEGRADED, UNAVAILABLE) and a SourceHealthResult dataclass (status, latency_ms, last_seen_path, error_message) in src/agent_recall/ingest/health.py
[DONE] Add a check_health() -> SourceHealthResult method to the base ingester interface in src/agent_recall/ingest/base.py
[DONE] Implement check_health() for each existing ingester (claude_code, cursor, file, etc.) — for file-based ingesters check path existence; for API-based ones attempt a lightweight HEAD/ping
[DONE] Update InteractiveSourcesWidget to display a colored status indicator (● green / ● yellow / ● red) next to each source name reflecting the last known HealthResult
[DONE] Add 'Ctrl+R' keybinding to re-run all health probes via WorkerMixin's worker thread; show a brief 'Checking...' spinner during the probe
[DONE] Persist the last health check result to .agent/source_health.json so it is available immediately on TUI start without re-probing
[DONE] Add the Ctrl+R hint to the sources view footer
acceptance_criteria: Each source in view:sources displays a color-coded health dot (green/yellow/red) based on the last health check result
Pressing Ctrl+R in view:sources triggers a re-probe of all sources; the dots update when probes complete
A 'Checking...' indicator is shown during an in-progress probe and disappears on completion
The health state is persisted between TUI sessions — the last known status is shown immediately on startup without waiting for a re-probe
Health check failures do not crash the TUI or block other panel interactions; errors are shown inline in the status indicator tooltip

items.title: Timeline Iteration Annotations
items.description: Extend the IterationDetailModal accessible from InteractiveTimelineWidget to allow the user to attach a short free-text annotation to any iteration. Annotations are stored as JSON sidecars next to the iteration diff files in .agent/ralph/iterations/. The timeline view renders an annotation indicator (📝) next to annotated iterations, and the modal displays the annotation text with an edit option.
items.user_story: As a developer reviewing past Ralph runs, I want to annotate specific iterations with notes about what I learned or why something failed, so I can build a retrospective knowledge trail directly inside the TUI.
steps: [DONE] Define an IterationAnnotation dataclass (iteration: int, text: str, created_at: datetime, updated_at: datetime) and add load_annotation / save_annotation methods to IterationReportStore
[DONE] Store annotations as .agent/ralph/iterations/{iteration:03d}.annotation.json sidecar files alongside the existing diff files
[DONE] Update IterationDetailModal to show the annotation text (or 'No annotation' placeholder) with an 'Add/Edit note' button (keybinding 'a')
[DONE] Pressing 'a' in the modal opens an inline text input; on confirm (Enter) the annotation is saved via save_annotation; on Escape the input is dismissed without saving
[DONE] Update InteractiveTimelineWidget to render a 📝 indicator next to iterations that have a non-empty annotation
[DONE] The annotation indicator should also appear in the timeline's iteration list panel, not just inside the modal
acceptance_criteria: Selecting an iteration in view:timeline and opening the detail modal shows any existing annotation or a 'No annotation' placeholder
Pressing 'a' in the modal opens a text input; submitting it saves the annotation to the correct sidecar file
After saving, the modal immediately reflects the new annotation text without requiring a TUI restart
Iterations with annotations display a 📝 indicator in the timeline list
Annotation files are correctly named {iteration:03d}.annotation.json and stored alongside diff files
Pressing Escape during text input cancels without overwriting an existing annotation

items.title: Context-Aware Ctrl+P Command Palette
items.description: Make the Ctrl+P command palette view-aware so it surfaces the most relevant actions first based on the currently active dashboard view. When on view:knowledge, knowledge-specific actions (edit, compact, export) appear at the top; on view:ralph, ralph-specific actions (run, pause, view-diff, select items) lead the list. Global actions remain available via normal search.
items.user_story: As a power user, I want the Ctrl+P palette to intelligently surface actions relevant to what I'm currently looking at, so I spend less time scrolling through a flat global action list.
steps: [DONE] Add a current_view: str property to the dashboard app that reflects the active named view (matches the existing view name registry)
[DONE] Refactor _build_command_suggestions in commands_mixin.py to accept an optional context_view: str parameter
[DONE] Define a VIEW_PRIORITY_MAP dict mapping view names to lists of action IDs that should be promoted to the top of the palette when that view is active

[DONE] When Ctrl+P is pressed, pass self.current_view to _build_command_suggestions; promoted actions are prepended before the global list with a visual separator (e.g. a dim '── current view ──' label row)
[DONE] Ensure the text search in the palette still searches the full global list regardless of the context filter
[DONE] Add view context labels to promoted actions (e.g. '[knowledge]' tag) to communicate why they appear at the top
[DONE] Update existing tests for command palette construction to cover context-aware promotion logic
acceptance_criteria: Opening Ctrl+P while on view:knowledge shows knowledge actions (edit entries, compact, export) at the top before global actions
Opening Ctrl+P while on view:ralph shows ralph actions (run loop, pause, view diff, select PRD items) at the top
Typing in the palette search box still returns global results — context promotion does not hide matching non-context actions
A visual separator is shown between context-promoted and global actions
The palette behaves identically to the current implementation when on a view with no defined context priorities (e.g. view:console)
All existing palette keyboard navigation (arrow keys, Enter, Escape) continues to work correctly

items.title: Add vector search to Storage interface and SQLite implementation
items.description: Extend the Storage abstract base class with a `search_chunks_by_embedding` method. Implement this method in `SQLiteStorage` leveraging the `sqlite-vec` extension for hardware-accelerated vector search. Update the `HTTPStorage` client to raise `NotImplementedError` temporarily. This establishes the pattern for server-side similarity search.
items.user_story: As a backend developer, I need to perform similarity searches directly within the storage layer so that I don't have to pull all chunks into memory to calculate cosine similarity.
steps: [DONE] Update `src/storage/base.py`: Add abstract method `search_chunks_by_embedding(embedding: list[float], limit: int = 10) -> list[ScoredChunk]`.

[DONE] Update `src/storage/sqlite.py`: Implement `search_chunks_by_embedding`. Ensure the SQL query utilizes `sqlite-vec` functions (e.g., `vec_distance_cosine` or `vec_top_k`) to rank results by proximity to the query vector.
[DONE] Update `src/storage/http.py`: Implement `search_chunks_by_embedding` to strictly raise `NotImplementedError`.
[DONE] Ensure `SQLiteStorage` initialization loads the `sqlite-vec` extension if not already loaded.
acceptance_criteria: `Storage` ABC requires `search_chunks_by_embedding` implementation.
`SQLiteStorage.search_chunks_by_embedding` returns a list of chunks sorted by similarity (descending score/ascending distance transformed to score).
`HTTPStorage.search_chunks_by_embedding` raises `NotImplementedError` when called.
No existing calls to `list_chunks_with_embeddings` are used for similarity purposes.
Tests confirm that searching with a known vector returns the expected nearest neighbor from a SQLite database.

items.title: Implement ScoredChunk model
items.description: Add a `ScoredChunk` model to `src/models.py`. This model should represent a `Chunk` coupled with a `score` (float) field. It will serve as the standardized return type for all embedding search operations across local and cloud backends.
items.user_story: As a developer, I need a unified data model for search results that includes similarity scores, so that the frontend and application logic can handle ranked results consistently regardless of the underlying storage engine.
steps: [DONE] Open `src/models.py`.
[DONE] Import the existing `Chunk` model.
[DONE] Define class `ScoredChunk` inheriting from `Chunk` (or composed of it, depending on existing Pydantic patterns).
[DONE] Add a field `score: float` to `ScoredChunk`.
[DONE] Ensure strict typing validation for the score field.
acceptance_criteria: `ScoredChunk` is available in `src/models.py`.
`ScoredChunk` validates that it contains all `Chunk` fields plus a float `score`.
`Storage.search_chunks_by_embedding` type hints are updated to return `list[ScoredChunk]`.
Serialization of `ScoredChunk` (e.g., `model_dump_json`) includes the score.

items.title: Decouple storage and indexing in ingest pipeline
items.description: Refactor the `store_chunk` usage in the ingestion pipeline logic. Instead of a single atomic call that implies both storage and indexing, split the operation into two distinct phases: first storing the chunk content (without embedding) and then indexing the vector. This prepares the codebase for split-backend architectures.
items.user_story: As a system architect, I want the ingest pipeline to treat content storage and vector indexing as separate operations so that we can eventually move vector indexing to a specialized service without rewriting the core loop.
steps: [DONE] Locate the main ingestion loop (likely in `src/ingest/` or `src/pipeline.py`).
[DONE] Identify where `storage.store_chunk(chunk)` is currently called.
[DONE] Modify the loop to create a copy of the chunk without the embedding: `chunk_no_vec = chunk.model_copy(update={'embedding': None})`.
[DONE] Call `storage.store_chunk(chunk_no_vec)` first.
[DONE] Immediately follow with a call to the embedding indexer: `storage.index(chunk.id, chunk.embedding)` (assuming `storage` currently implements the `EmbeddingBackend` interface or similar).
[DONE] If `storage.index` does not exist, expose the vector insertion logic of `SQLiteStorage` as a public method or separate interface method.
acceptance_criteria: The ingest pipeline makes two separate calls per chunk processing iteration.
The first call to `store_chunk` passes a chunk object where `embedding` is None or omitted.
The second call explicitly passes the ID and Vector to an indexing method.
Data integrity is maintained: the resulting database still contains both the chunk text and the vector.
Refactor is a 'no-op' in terms of system behavior/output.

items.title: Add storage replication utility
items.description: Create a standalone utility function `replicate_chunks_to` in `src/storage/utils.py`. This function takes a source `Storage` and a target `Storage` and copies chunks from source to target. This serves as the primitive for 'export to cloud' features.
items.user_story: As a user, I want to be able to sync my local knowledge base to a cloud environment or share it with a team member, requiring a reliable mechanism to copy data between storage instances.
steps: Create `src/storage/utils.py` if it does not exist.
Define `def replicate_chunks_to(source: Storage, target: Storage) -> int:`.
Implement the logic: Iterate through chunks in `source` (using `list_chunks` or an iterator).
For each chunk, call `target.store_chunk(chunk)`.
Ideally, handle batching if the Storage API supports it.
Return the count of replicated chunks.
acceptance_criteria: `replicate_chunks_to` is importable from `src.storage.utils`.
Function accepts any two implementations of the `Storage` ABC (e.g., SQLite to SQLite, or SQLite to HTTP).
All chunks present in the source are upserted into the target.
Embeddings and metadata are preserved during replication.
The function returns the total number of chunks processed.
